signing_authority: Not disclosed (name/designation fields left blank in report)

system_description.overview: Filed is a tax preparation SaaS platform that uses AI to automate and accelerate the tax return preparation lifecycle for accounting firms. It delivers AI-driven document ingestion, organization, anomaly detection, and integration with existing tax software, helping tax professionals streamline low-value tasks.

products.Filed SaaS Platform: Automates tax return prep lifecycle with AI-driven document ingestion, data extraction, anomaly detection, and workflow automation for accounting firms
products.Automated Compliance Management Platform: End-to-end compliance automation for SOC 2, HIPAA, ISO 27001, GDPR, PCI DSS with continuous monitoring and automated evidence collection (NOTE: appears to be template/boilerplate text left in report)
products.AI-Powered Security and Compliance Featu: Automated code scanning, infrastructure scanning, access control monitoring, security questionnaire automation (NOTE: appears to be template/boilerplate text)

infrastructure.physical_presence: No physical offices in scope; ~30 employees in Brooklyn, NY and Stockholm, Sweden

network_architecture.network_segmentation: Network topology includes segmented VPCs and access control lists (ACLs). Network segmentation implemented to separate systems and data based on sensitivity and criticality.
network_architecture.api_gateway: Application gateway mentioned (firewalls configured on application gateway)
network_architecture.firewalls: System firewalls configured on application gateway and production network to limit unnecessary ports, protocols, and services. Firewall rules reviewed annually by IT management.
network_architecture.ids_ips: IDS and IPS via Azure to identify and protect against threats in conjunction with security policy network settings
network_architecture.vpn: Remote access done through a bastion host with VPN. Network diagram shows VPC VPN Gateways and IPSec VPN Tunnel.
network_architecture.tls: TLS used for encrypting user requests to web-based systems, using certificates from established third-party CA
network_architecture.content_filtering: Content filtering system routed through proxy server
network_architecture.topology: Internet -> VPC VPN Connection -> VPE WEST and VPE EAST regions with Internet Gateways -> Multiple Availability Zones with VPC subnet private segments -> Production HA, Dev, Test, D.com environments -> VPC VPN Gateways -> IPSec VPN Tunnel to controllers. NOTE: Network diagram is a SAMPLE template placeholder - not Filed's actual diagram.

application_architecture.environments: Separate development, test/stage, and production environments mentioned (CC8.1.4)

databases: Azure managed databases (specific type not disclosed)

authentication_access_control.identity_provider: Azure Active Directory (implied by Password policy of Azure and AD references)
authentication_access_control.mfa: MFA enforced for administrative access to production platform and for all remote access. MFA required for source code tool access. Two-factor authentication required for employee email.
authentication_access_control.password_policy: Controlled through Azure password policy including periodic forced changes, password expiry, complexity requirements. Accounts disabled after limited unsuccessful logon attempts. Guest and anonymous logins not allowed. Desktops locked after inactivity.

encryption.at_rest: Cloud provider key management service (Azure KMS)
encryption.key_management: Azure cloud provider key management service; access to encryption keys restricted to authorized individuals

ci_cd_devops.source_control: Source code management tool (not named; MFA-protected access, changes logged and time-stamped)
ci_cd_devops.branch_protection: Code repository branch rules configured to ensure every merge request to move change to production requires additional approval. Developers cannot make changes to production code without approval.
ci_cd_devops.iac: Baseline configurations retained as infrastructure as code; configuration management tool used for implementation and rollback
ci_cd_devops.patch_management: Automatic patch management with monthly reviews by CTO. Workstations configured for automatic updates and scanned daily for patch compliance. OS patches tested before production deployment.

monitoring_logging.apm_tool: Not specifically named; monitoring tools used to continuously monitor system capacity and performance
monitoring_logging.log_aggregation: Logging enabled at application and infrastructure level for admin activities, logon attempts, data deletions, function changes, security configurations, permissions, and roles
monitoring_logging.alerting: Automated alerts configured for predefined thresholds and high/critical risk events notifying IT management

third_party_services.purpose: Cloud infrastructure and data center services (subservice organization)

security_tools.antivirus: Endpoint Security Solution with anti-virus, anti-malware, and Trojan protection installed on all production servers and Mac workstations/laptops. Auto-scan on continuous basis, definitions updated weekly for servers and daily from vendor for endpoints.
security_tools.vulnerability_scanning: Monthly automated vulnerability scans on infrastructure and applications, frequency adjusted as needed. Done by internal IT Infrastructure team.
security_tools.penetration_testing: Annual external penetration testing by independent third-party. High/critical findings remediated and added to risk register.

untestable: CC2.2.3 - No significant changes to people/roles/responsibilities during engagement period
CC3.2.1 - No cyber incidents reported during engagement period (cybersecurity insurance control)
CC6.5.2 - No customer terminations during engagement period (data deletion control)
CC7.3.1 - No security incidents reported during engagement period
excluded_criteria: CC6.4 - Physical access controls excluded; Azure responsible for data center physical security
A1.2 - Environmental protections excluded; Filed has no office/physical servers, managed by building management/Azure
Processing Integrity - Determined not relevant to the system
Privacy - Determined not relevant to the system
notable_controls: CC8.1.2 - Branch protection with merge request approval required for production deployments
CC6.2.2 - Quarterly user access reviews including administrative privilege accounts
CC6.1.4 - Access revocation within one business day of termination
CC7.4.1 - BCDR plans tested annually with multiple loss scenarios
CC4.1.1 - Annual external penetration testing by independent third-party
CC7.1.2 - Monthly automated vulnerability scanning with remediation tracking
CC9.2.1 - Annual SOC report review for vendors; quarterly risk assessment for critical vendors without SOC reports
CC3.1.1 - IT Leadership Committee meets weekly to review security commitment activities

infrastructure.rationale: Multi-region (3 Azure regions) with multi-AZ is solid. IaC mentioned for baseline configs. However, no CDN, no named serverless or container orchestration, and the network diagram is a placeholder template rather than Filed's actual architecture - a significant concern about report quality.

application_architecture.rationale: Almost no application architecture details disclosed. No languages, frameworks, or specific compute services named. Pattern not stated. Only basic facts: web interface, separate environments, and branch protection. Very limited technical depth.

data_layer.rationale: Daily backups with multi-AZ confirmed. Data classification and retention policies in place. Per-tenant segregation mentioned. However, no specific database technologies named, no caching layer, no data warehouse, and no details on backup encryption or recovery testing specifics.

security_posture.rationale: Comprehensive security controls: MFA everywhere, IDS/IPS, firewalls, antivirus/anti-malware on servers and endpoints, monthly vulnerability scanning, annual pen testing, cybersecurity insurance, quarterly access reviews, incident response with root cause analysis. Deducted for no WAF mentioned, no named SIEM tool, and no specific vulnerability scanning tool named.

devops_maturity.rationale: IaC for baseline configurations, branch protection with merge approval, separate environments, formal change management with emergency process. However, no named CI/CD tool, no named source control platform, no container orchestration, and no automated testing pipeline details.

bcdr_readiness.rationale: BCDR policy documented and reviewed annually. Annual testing across multiple loss scenarios. Daily backups with multi-AZ. DR plan includes roles, responsibilities, and communication plans. However, no stated RTO/RPO, no multi-region failover confirmed, and DR plan details are in Section 5 (not audited).

vendor_diversity.rationale: Only one vendor (Microsoft Azure) identified as subservice organization. No other third-party services named in the report. Extreme single-vendor concentration risk. Vendor management program exists but vendor inventory not disclosed.

overall.rationale: Filed passes SOC 2 Type 2 with zero exceptions across 67 controls, which is positive. Multi-region Azure deployment with multi-AZ, strong access controls, and comprehensive security policies. However, the report suffers from heavy use of template/boilerplate text (sample network diagram, sample org chart, template product descriptions left in), extremely low technical specificity (no named tools, languages, or frameworks), and single-vendor dependency. The 3-month audit period is very short. Overall: governance and process controls are solid, but technical depth and transparency are below expectations.

red_flags: Network diagram is a SAMPLE PLACEHOLDER (yellow sticky note says 'Please replace this with your actual network diagram') - not Filed's actual architecture
Organization chart is also a SAMPLE PLACEHOLDER with instructions to replace
Products in Scope section contains template instructions text: '(Instructions: Modify this text to provide more context...)'

Products in Scope describes an 'Automated Compliance Management Platform' and 'AI-Powered Security and Compliance Features' which appear to be boilerplate from the auditor's template, not Filed's actual products
Signing Authority Name and Designation fields are blank in the Management Assertion (only 'Your Name Here' placeholder)
Processing Integrity and Privacy trust criteria excluded despite handling sensitive tax and financial data
No specific technology stack details disclosed anywhere in the report (no named databases, languages, frameworks, CI/CD tools)
yellow_flags: Single cloud vendor dependency (Azure only) with no disclosed multi-cloud or failover strategy beyond Azure regions
No WAF mentioned despite being a web-based SaaS application
No named SIEM, APM, or log aggregation tool
No named source control platform or CI/CD pipeline tool
No RTO/RPO disclosed in BCDR plans
Only ~30 employees - small team size relative to scope of compliance commitments
Vulnerability scanning described as internal only (by internal IT Infrastructure team) - external scanning coverage unclear
4 controls could not be tested for operating effectiveness due to no events during the period
green_flags: Zero exceptions across all 67 controls tested
Multi-region Azure deployment across 3 US regions with multi-AZ
MFA enforced comprehensively: production admin access, remote access, source code tools, and employee email
Quarterly user access reviews including administrative privileges
Access revocation within one business day of termination
Annual external penetration testing by independent third-party
Monthly automated vulnerability scanning with remediation tracking
Formal BCDR plans tested annually across multiple loss scenarios
Branch protection requiring merge approval before production deployment
Annual vendor SOC report reviews with quarterly risk assessment for critical vendors without SOC
IT Leadership Committee meets weekly to review security operations
key_observations: This report has extensive template/boilerplate artifacts that were never replaced with Filed-specific content, strongly suggesting it was prepared using an automated compliance platform with insufficient customization

The mismatch between the Products in Scope (compliance automation platform, code scanning) and the actual System Description (tax preparation SaaS) suggests the template text was not reviewed carefully
Despite these quality issues, the actual controls tested appear substantive and all passed without exceptions
The auditor (Accorp Partners) appears to be a lesser-known firm, which combined with the template issues raises questions about audit rigor

Filed's AI/ML capabilities (document ingestion, anomaly detection, data extraction) are core product features but receive no technical detail in the report regarding how they are implemented or secured
The report references a CISO role (CC2.1.4/CC7.4) but the org chart shows only CEO, CTO, COO, CRO as C-suite - the CISO role may be a combined responsibility
Tax data is highly sensitive (PII, financial data) but Privacy trust criterion was excluded from scope
